test_result.details: Hapax ratio of 0.701 is incompatible with coherent narrative structure, which requires semantic repetition of themes and concepts; the Zipf exponent supports natural language patterns, but vocabulary-to-total ratio (21.7%) and high lexical diversity contradict thematic coherence.

test_result.test: claude_evaluation
test_result.details: A null-derived prefix system with 5 glyphs accounting for >60% tokens should produce a steep Zipf exponent >1.5 through concentration; the observed 0.8946 indicates shallow uniform distribution contradicting prefix dominance, confirmed by the 70.1% hapax ratio showing vocabulary is highly diverse rather than concentrated in prefix-stem combinations.
